message:
feat(runtime,server): implement cross-client Pub/Sub message delivery

Major parity milestone: Pub/Sub messages now route between different
client connections, matching Redis behavior.

Architecture:
- Add global PubSubRegistry to ServerState: channel→clients, pattern→clients,
  shard→clients mappings, plus per-client outbox for pending messages
- SUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE/SSUBSCRIBE register in global registry (by client_id)
- PUBLISH walks global registry, queues PubSubMessage in each subscriber's
  outbox (supports both direct channel and pattern matches)
- Server event loop runs deliver_pubsub_messages() sweep after each tick,
  writing queued messages to each subscribed client's write buffer

Changes:
- Move SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE/PUNSUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH/SSUBSCRIBE/
  SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH from fr-command dispatch to runtime-special commands
  so they have access to the global subscription state
- Add pubsub_cleanup_client() called on disconnect to remove all
  subscriptions for departing clients
- Make ClientSession.client_id public for server-side client lookup
- Add deliver_pubsub_messages() to server event loop for cross-client
  message delivery via mio write buffer + WRITABLE interest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
